item.title: cmd_clean prints success even when remove_items_by_status fails
item.intent: cmd_clean must not report successful cleanup if the backlog file was not actually updated — silent data loss on I/O error is worse than a visible failure.
item.description: In cmd_clean(), remove_items_by_status() is called but its return code is not checked. If jq fails (malformed JSON, disk full) or the mv that replaces the file fails, the command continues and prints 'Cleaned N features/bugs' as though it succeeded. The user believes the backlog was cleaned when it was not. Fix: check return code of both remove_items_by_status calls and exit non-zero with a clear error if either fails.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_clean() and locate the two remove_items_by_status calls (around line 4067-4068)
Capture the return code of each call: 'remove_items_by_status ... || { log_error "Failed to clean backlog.json"; return 1; }'
Ensure the success message is only printed after both calls succeed
Verify: run clean on a valid setup (success printed), then test with a read-only backlog file (error printed, no success message)
acceptanceCriteria: cmd_clean exits non-zero and prints an error when remove_items_by_status fails
cmd_clean success message is only printed when cleanup actually succeeds
cmd_clean --dry-run is not affected (no file writes in dry-run path)
